summary: mypy type checker reports 641 errors (298 unique) across pandas modules, predominantly in arrow-related array code, with repeated 'Module has no attribute' errors indicating type information is unavailable or incomplete.
root_cause: mypy cannot resolve module attributes, likely due to missing or unavailable type stubs for optional dependencies.

evidence.reason: Type checker failed with a large number of type errors

evidence.quote: pandas/core/arrays/arrow/array.py:2708: error: Module has no attribu...
evidence.reason: Shows repeated 'Module has no attribute' pattern across multiple lines in the same file, characteristic of type stub or import resolution issues

evidence.quote: Check import. No warnings, and blocklist some optional dependencies
evidence.reason: Warning message suggests optional dependencies may not be available or properly configured for type checking

suggested_fix: Ensure all optional dependencies required for type checking are installed and have type stubs available. Verify mypy configuration includes proper paths to installed packages.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
